Content:
Run your trading account like a business — or the market will run it for you.

Before I was a trader, I was a businessman. Five years of payroll, suppliers, customers. I knew money was made the slow way.

Then I forgot all of it the moment someone promised me easy money. It cost me three years of company profit.

So this channel is built the way I should have traded from day one:

Every signal has a stop loss.
I post my losses, not just my wins.
I never promise profit.

That's it. No hype. If you want a track record instead of a story, follow along.

The most expensive sentence in trading is only three words long.

"Fixed returns. Zero risk."

A man said that to me once. He didn't show me charts — he showed me clean, beautiful numbers. In my company I audited every invoice and read every contract twice. But that one sentence walked straight past five years of business instinct, because it promised the one thing business never gave me: certainty.

I handed him three years of profit. A few months later the numbers were gone, and so was he.

If anyone ever promises you fixed, guaranteed, or risk-free — walk away. That's the whole lesson, and it cost me everything to learn it.

The full story: https://rextradingsignal.com/about.html

Rule 01 of this channel: every signal has a stop loss. Before we ever talk about profit.

Why so strict? Because capital is oxygen. No oxygen, no next trade.

A stop loss isn't admitting defeat. It's the price of staying in the game long enough for your edge to show up. The trader who survives a bad week is the one still here for the good one.

I learned this the hard way — my first blown account had no stop loss, because a stop "felt like giving up." The market gave up on me instead.

Decide what you're willing to lose before you click. Every time.

Most traders set risk trade by trade, in the heat of the moment. That's backwards.

Set your Risk Ceiling first — a monthly loss cap you decide while you're calm. "This month, I will not lose more than X." Then cascade it down: per week, per day, per trade.

Now every position size answers to one number you chose with a clear head, not to how you feel after two losses.

The market can't blow your account past a ceiling you set in advance. But you can — if you never set one.

How the Risk Ceiling works: https://rextradingsignal.com/how-to-manage-risk-in-gold-trading.html

Position sizing sounds like maths homework. It's one division.

Take the amount you're willing to lose on a trade. Divide it by the distance to your stop loss. That's your size. Done.

Here's the part beginners get backwards: a WIDER stop means a SMALLER trade, not a bigger one. More room for the market to breathe = fewer units, so your dollar risk stays fixed.

Get this one habit right and you'll never again "accidentally" risk half your account on a single gold trade.

Position sizing without the maths headache: https://rextradingsignal.com/position-sizing-for-gold-trading.html

Why do most traders blow their accounts? It's almost never the strategy.

I burned four accounts finding out. Four different ways to lose:

No plan — I bought gold because it "looked strong."
Revenge — I doubled size after every loss to get back to even faster.
The guru — I followed a man who only posted his winners, blind.
Overconfidence — a few good months, so I sized up until one news spike erased six.

Different mistakes, one root cause: I traded my emotions instead of a written rule.

The strategy was never the problem. The discipline was.

The four failure patterns, in full: https://rextradingsignal.com/why-do-most-traders-blow-their-accounts.html

Rule 02: I post my losses.

A channel that only shows winners is showing you a story, not a track record. Anyone can screenshot green days and hide the red ones.

So you get the whole notebook. Losing trades included — each one small, each one with a stop loss that fired exactly when it should have. That's not failure. That's the system working.

A green week isn't a week with no losses. It's a week where the losses stayed small and the winners were allowed to run.

Live signals — wins and losses, out loud — are posted in our Telegram: https://t.me/+CR21qcOU0QI4ZDg9

Treat trading like a business and most of your problems disappear.

A business has: a written plan, a spending limit, a ledger, and a review at the end of the day. Trading needs the exact same four:

A written trade plan — so you're not improvising with money.
A risk ceiling — your spending limit, decided while calm.
A trade journal — your ledger of what actually happened.
A nightly audit — the review that turns mistakes into lessons.

The moment I ran my trades the way I ran my company, the account stopped feeling like a casino.

The full operator's system: https://rextradingsignal.com/treat-trading-like-a-business.html

The cheapest edge in trading is a notebook. Almost nobody uses it.

A trade journal isn't a diary of feelings. It's a ledger: entry, exit, size, stop, the setup, and — the field everyone skips — did I follow my own plan, yes or no?

Log that honestly for a month and a pattern appears. Your biggest losses will nearly all say "no." That's the audit doing its job: it shows you that your problem isn't finding better trades, it's obeying the ones you already planned.

You can't fix what you never wrote down.

The journal template and how to audit it: https://rextradingsignal.com/trading-journal-template.html

Account number two died of revenge.

After a loss, I doubled my size to get back to even faster. Then doubled again. Three trades — that's all it took to close the account.

Revenge trading feels like fighting back. It's actually the market handing you a bigger shovel.

Here's the fix, and it's boring: your size never changes because of the last trade's result. Same risk when you're up, same risk when you're down. The trade in front of you doesn't know or care what the last one did.

Flat, unemotional size is how an account survives a losing streak — and every trader gets one.

Rule 03: I never promise profit. Here's why I can't, and won't.

Account number three died because I paid a "guru" who only ever posted his winners. I followed him blindly. When the losing streak came — it always comes — I had no idea why I was in any of my trades, so I couldn't manage a single one.

A track record you can't see through is worthless. Promises of "guaranteed" and "fixed" are the loudest sign someone is hiding the losing half.

So I'll say it plainly: I don't know if the next trade wins. Nobody does. What I can promise is a stop loss on every one, and the losses posted alongside the wins.

That's the only honest deal in this business.

The account that hurt most was the one where I was trading BETTER.

A few good months. My analysis was sharper, my entries cleaner. So I raised my size far beyond what the account could carry — because clearly I'd figured it out.

One news spike took back six months of work in a single night.

The market wasn't testing my charts. It was testing whether I'd respect my own rules when I felt invincible. I failed.

Now I size the same whether I'm up 30% or down. Boring? Yes. Still here? Also yes.

Your edge isn't your best month. It's whether you can survive your worst one.

Your entire trading business can fit on one page. Mine does.

Six fields, and you can fill them tonight:

Mission — why you trade, in one honest line.
Risk ceiling — the most you'll lose this month.
Trade ticket — what a valid setup looks like before you click.
Journal — where every trade gets logged.
Nightly audit — the five-minute review that catches repeats.
And a hard rule for when to stop.

No 40-page strategy PDF. One page you'll actually use beats a system you never open.

Grab the free one-page template and business plan: https://rextradingsignal.com/business-plan/

Most of what I do never makes it to this page.

The daily gold setups, the live stop-loss management, the "should I close this now?" moments — that all happens inside our Telegram channel, in real time.

It's not for everyone. If you want hype and "guaranteed profit," you'll hate it there. We don't do that.

But if you're serious about protecting your capital and trading with rules instead of fear, the door is open. Every signal comes with a stop loss. The losses get posted too. Nothing is promised.
